title: Touchpad or Keypad Short Circuit
shortDescription: The oven control panel has detected a short circuit or stuck key on the touchpad membrane.
fullDescription:
Error code F1 on LG ovens indicates that the electronic control board has detected a short circuit or continuously pressed key signal from the touchpad membrane. The touchpad is a thin flexible circuit behind the front control panel that registers button presses.

Over time, moisture, heat, or physical wear can cause the membrane layers to stick together, simulating a permanently pressed button. Grease and cleaning liquids that seep behind the panel can also cause shorts between the membrane traces.

In some cases, F1 is caused by a faulty control board rather than the touchpad itself. If replacing the touchpad does not resolve the error, the control board is likely the culprit.

causes.cause: Stuck key or short circuit in touchpad membrane
Moisture or grease behind the touchpad panel
Damaged ribbon cable between touchpad and control board

symptoms: F1 error displayed on oven panel
Oven beeps continuously
Control panel buttons do not respond
Oven may turn on or off unexpectedly

fixSteps.title: Power reset the oven
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the oven at the circuit breaker for 2 minutes. Restore power and check if F1 clears. This can resolve temporary glitches from moisture or static.

fixSteps.title: Inspect for moisture
fixSteps.instruction: If the oven was recently cleaned with spray cleaner, moisture may have entered behind the panel. Allow the oven to sit unpowered for 24 hours to let any moisture evaporate.

fixSteps.title: Check the ribbon cable
fixSteps.instruction: If comfortable, remove the rear panel or access the control area. Disconnect and reseat the flat ribbon cable between the touchpad and the control board.

fixSteps.title: Replace the touchpad
fixSteps.instruction: Order a replacement touchpad overlay for your model. Peel off the old membrane and carefully apply the new one, connecting the ribbon cable.

whenToCallPro: If replacing the touchpad does not fix F1, the control board needs replacement, which is best done by a technician.

faq.q: Can I still use my oven with F1?
faq.a: No. The oven may behave unpredictably with a stuck key signal. Disconnect power until the issue is resolved.

faq.q: How do I avoid F1 errors?
faq.a: Do not spray cleaning products directly on the control panel. Wipe with a damp cloth instead.

title: Open or Shorted Oven Temperature Sensor
shortDescription: The oven temperature sensor (RTD probe) circuit is open or shorted, preventing temperature monitoring.
fullDescription:
Error code F3 indicates that the oven temperature sensor has failed. This sensor, also called an RTD (Resistance Temperature Detector), is a probe that extends into the oven cavity and monitors the internal temperature. The control board reads the sensor's resistance to determine the oven temperature.

When the sensor wire breaks (open circuit) or the sensor shorts internally, the control board cannot read the oven temperature and displays F3. The oven will not heat because it cannot regulate temperature safely without this sensor.

The oven temperature sensor is one of the most commonly replaced parts on ovens. It is typically located at the back of the oven cavity, held in place by one or two screws, and connects to the control board via a wire harness.

causes.cause: Sensor wire connector loose at control board

symptoms: F3 error displayed on oven panel
Oven will not heat
Oven shuts off during preheating
Temperature reading shows extremely high or low value

fixSteps.title: Power reset
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the oven breaker for 2 minutes, then restore power. If F3 returns immediately, the sensor needs testing.

fixSteps.title: Test the sensor resistance
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the sensor harness at the back of the oven (or at the control board). Using a multimeter, measure the sensor resistance. At room temperature (70°F/21°C), it should read approximately 1080-1090 ohms. An open (infinite) or zero reading confirms a bad sensor.

fixSteps.title: Replace the oven temperature sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the 1-2 screws holding the sensor probe inside the oven cavity (accessible from inside the oven). Pull the sensor out through the back wall. Disconnect the old sensor and connect the new one. Feed it back through the hole and secure with screws.

fixSteps.title: Test the oven
fixSteps.instruction: Restore power and set the oven to 350°F. Verify it reaches temperature without triggering F3.

whenToCallPro: If the new sensor also produces F3, the control board's sensor circuit is faulty and needs professional diagnosis.

faq.q: How much does an oven temperature sensor cost?
faq.a: LG oven temperature sensors typically cost $10–$20 and are one of the easiest oven repairs.

faq.q: What should the sensor resistance be?
faq.a: At room temperature (about 70°F), a standard oven RTD sensor reads approximately 1080 ohms. It increases with temperature.

title: Oven Control Board Relay Failure
shortDescription: A relay on the oven control board has stuck or failed, preventing proper element control.
fullDescription:
Error code F5 indicates that a relay on the electronic oven control board has malfunctioned. The control board uses relays to switch the bake and broil heating elements on and off. When a relay welds shut (stuck closed) or fails to close, the control board detects the issue and displays F5.

A stuck-closed relay is particularly dangerous because it means the heating element cannot be turned off by the control board. The oven may overheat. A stuck-open relay means the element will not heat at all.

This error typically requires replacement of the control board, as individual relays on the board are surface-mounted and difficult to replace without specialized soldering equipment.

causes.cause: Welded (stuck closed) relay on control board
Loose wiring at control board relay terminals

symptoms: F5 error on display
Oven overheats and does not turn off
Oven does not heat at all
Burning smell from oven even when off

fixSteps.title: Disconnect power immediately
fixSteps.instruction: If the oven is overheating, turn off the circuit breaker immediately. A stuck relay can cause the element to stay on continuously, which is a fire hazard.

fixSteps.title: Let the oven cool
fixSteps.instruction: Wait for the oven to cool completely before attempting any inspection.

fixSteps.title: Verify the error
fixSteps.instruction: Restore power briefly to confirm F5 is displayed. Then disconnect power again.

fixSteps.title: Replace the control board
fixSteps.instruction: Order a replacement electronic control board for your model. Remove the back panel, photograph all wiring connections, disconnect the old board, and install the new one. Reconnect all wires matching your photo.

whenToCallPro: If you are uncomfortable working with high-voltage oven wiring, or if the oven was overheating, call a professional for safety.

faq.q: Is F5 a fire hazard?
faq.a: Potentially yes. If a relay is stuck closed, the heating element cannot be turned off. Disconnect power immediately and do not use the oven until repaired.

faq.q: Can the control board be repaired instead of replaced?
faq.a: Some appliance repair services offer board-level repair where the relay is replaced. This is cheaper but requires soldering skills and may not be warrantied.

title: Door Lock Motor Failure
shortDescription: The oven door lock motor has failed to lock or unlock the door within the expected time.
fullDescription:
Error code F7 indicates a malfunction of the oven door lock mechanism. The door lock is used during the self-clean cycle to keep the door securely closed while the oven reaches extremely high temperatures (up to 900°F). The lock motor must complete its locking or unlocking motion within a set time.

If the lock motor is stuck, the lock switch is misaligned, or the motor has burned out, the control board generates F7. This error can occur at the start of a self-clean cycle (door won't lock) or at the end (door won't unlock).

A common scenario is the door remaining locked after a self-clean cycle due to a failed lock motor. Do not force the door open — this can break the lock mechanism or the door hinges.

causes.cause: Lock mechanism jammed with food debris
Wiring issue between lock motor and control board

symptoms: F7 error on display
Door will not lock for self-clean
Door remains locked after self-clean cycle
Clicking sound from lock mechanism area

fixSteps.title: Power reset
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the breaker for 5 minutes. This may reset the lock motor and allow the door to unlock if stuck.

fixSteps.title: Wait for cool-down
fixSteps.instruction: If the oven was in self-clean mode, the door will not unlock until the temperature drops below 550°F. Wait 1-2 hours for full cool-down.

fixSteps.title: Manually release the lock
fixSteps.instruction: If the door is stuck locked and the oven is cool, access the lock mechanism from behind the control panel or through the bottom drawer opening. Look for a manual release lever and slide it to unlock.

fixSteps.title: Replace the lock motor
fixSteps.instruction: If the lock motor has failed, order a replacement. Disconnect power, remove the back panel or top, and replace the lock motor assembly.

whenToCallPro: If the door is stuck locked and you cannot access the manual release, call a technician to avoid damaging the door or lock.
videoSearchQuery: LG oven F7 door lock error fix

faq.q: My oven door is locked and will not open. What do I do?
faq.a: First, turn off the breaker for 5 minutes and wait for the oven to cool completely. If still locked, try to access the manual lock release.

faq.q: Can I use the oven normally if F7 appears?
faq.a: You can use bake and broil functions, but the self-clean cycle will not work until the lock motor is replaced.

title: Door Lock Switch Error
shortDescription: The door lock switch is not reporting the correct door lock status to the control board.
fullDescription:
Error code F9 indicates a mismatch between the expected and actual door lock position. The control board monitors the door lock switch to confirm whether the door is locked or unlocked. If the switch signal does not match the expected state after the lock motor has been activated, F9 is generated.

This can occur if the lock switch is out of alignment, the switch itself has failed, or the wiring between the switch and control board is broken. It can also appear if the lock motor (F7) is trying to lock but cannot reach full engagement.

F9 is closely related to F7 — both involve the door lock mechanism, but F9 specifically relates to the position feedback switch rather than the motor itself.

causes.cause: Door lock switch misaligned or failed
Wiring break between switch and control board
Door hinge sag preventing proper alignment

symptoms: F9 error on display
Self-clean cycle will not start
Door lock engages but error still appears
Intermittent error during self-clean

fixSteps.title: Power reset
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the breaker for 2 minutes, then restore power.

fixSteps.title: Check door alignment
fixSteps.instruction: Close the oven door firmly. If the door does not close flush, the hinges may be worn or bent, preventing the lock from engaging fully.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the lock switch
fixSteps.instruction: Access the lock assembly behind the control panel. Check that the switch is properly positioned and that its lever makes contact with the lock bar when the motor engages.

fixSteps.title: Replace the lock assembly
fixSteps.instruction: If the switch is faulty, replace the entire lock motor assembly, which includes the switch.

whenToCallPro: If the door alignment or hinges are the root cause, professional adjustment may be needed.
videoSearchQuery: LG oven F9 door lock switch error fix

faq.q: Is F9 the same as F7?
faq.a: They are related but different. F7 is the lock motor not completing its motion. F9 is the lock position switch not confirming the correct state.

faq.q: Can I skip self-clean and just use the oven normally?
faq.a: Yes. F9 only affects the self-clean cycle. Normal baking and broiling should still work.

title: Communication Error Between Control Boards
shortDescription: The main control board and the user interface board are not communicating properly.
fullDescription:
Error code F11 indicates a communication failure between the oven's two main electronic components: the user interface (UI) board behind the front panel and the main control board (relay board) that operates the heating elements. These boards communicate via a serial data line.

When the data connection is broken, the oven cannot translate button presses into heating actions. The most common cause is a loose or damaged ribbon cable or wire harness between the two boards. Power surges can also damage the communication circuitry on either board.

This error sometimes occurs intermittently, especially if a connector is making poor contact due to heat-related expansion and contraction.

causes.cause: Loose ribbon cable or wire harness between boards

symptoms: F11 error on display
Oven buttons respond but elements do not heat
Error appears intermittently
Display may flicker or show garbled text

fixSteps.title: Power reset
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the breaker for 5 minutes. Restore power and see if F11 clears.

fixSteps.title: Reseat cable connections
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power. Remove the back panel or access the control area. Disconnect and firmly reseat all ribbon cables and wire harness connectors between the UI board and the main board.

fixSteps.title: Inspect for damage
fixSteps.instruction: Look for burn marks, corrosion, or melted pins on connectors. Check for cracked solder joints on either board.

fixSteps.title: Replace the faulty board
fixSteps.instruction: If reseating connectors does not help, one of the boards needs replacement. Try the UI board first as it is usually cheaper. If that does not fix it, the main control board is likely the issue.

whenToCallPro: If reseating cables does not resolve F11, determining which board is faulty requires diagnostic testing that is best done by a technician.
videoSearchQuery: LG oven F11 communication error fix

faq.q: How do I know which board is bad?
faq.a: Without diagnostic equipment, it is difficult. The UI board is more commonly the culprit and is cheaper to try first.

faq.q: Can a power surge cause F11?
faq.a: Yes. A surge protector for your kitchen circuit is recommended to prevent this type of damage.

title: Convection Fan Motor Error
shortDescription: The convection fan motor is not running or has failed to reach the expected speed.
fullDescription:
Error code F19 indicates that the convection fan motor has malfunctioned. In convection ovens, a fan at the back of the oven cavity circulates hot air for more even cooking. The control board monitors the fan's operation and generates F19 when the fan fails to spin.

A burned-out fan motor is the most common cause. Food splatter or grease buildup on the fan blades can also overload the motor over time. In some cases, the fan blade itself may have come loose from the motor shaft.

The oven can still be used in non-convection (standard bake/broil) mode while awaiting repair. Only the convection function is affected.

causes.cause: Grease buildup on fan blades causing motor strain
Loose fan blade on motor shaft

symptoms: F19 error when selecting convection mode
No fan noise from back of oven during convection
Uneven cooking or longer cooking times
Oven works fine in standard bake mode

fixSteps.title: Power reset
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the breaker for 2 minutes and restore power. Try convection mode again.

fixSteps.title: Listen for the fan
fixSteps.instruction: Set the oven to convection mode. Listen at the back of the oven for the fan. If you hear nothing, the motor is likely dead. If you hear humming but no spinning, the fan blade may be jammed.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the fan
fixSteps.instruction: With the oven off and cool, remove the rear interior panel (usually held by a few screws) to access the convection fan. Check for loose fan blade, grease buildup, or debris blocking rotation.

fixSteps.title: Replace the convection fan motor
fixSteps.instruction: From the back of the oven (exterior), remove the rear panel to access the motor. Disconnect the wiring, remove mounting screws, and install the new motor. Reattach the fan blade.

whenToCallPro: If you cannot access the rear of the oven (built-in wall ovens), a technician can more easily handle the motor replacement.
videoSearchQuery: LG oven F19 convection fan error fix

faq.q: Can I bake without the convection fan?
faq.a: Yes. Standard bake and broil modes do not use the convection fan. Only the convection function is affected.

faq.q: How do I clean the convection fan?
faq.a: Remove the interior back panel with the oven off and cool. Wipe the fan blades with a damp cloth and mild degreaser.

title: Upper Oven Temperature Exceeded Limit
shortDescription: The upper oven cavity temperature has exceeded the maximum safe threshold.
fullDescription:
Error code F21 indicates that the upper oven cavity has reached a temperature beyond the maximum safe limit set by the control board. This is a safety shutoff to prevent damage to the oven, surrounding cabinetry, or potential fire.

The most common cause is a stuck relay on the control board that keeps the heating element energized even after the setpoint is reached. A failed temperature sensor that reads lower than actual temperature can also cause overshoot. In double ovens, F21 specifically refers to the upper cavity.

This is a serious safety error. Disconnect power immediately when F21 appears.

causes.cause: Stuck relay on control board keeping element on
Failed temperature sensor reading too low

symptoms: F21 error on display
Oven is extremely hot — much hotter than set temperature
Burning smell
Oven shuts off automatically

fixSteps.title: Disconnect power immediately
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the circuit breaker. Do not attempt to use the oven. Allow it to cool completely.

fixSteps.title: Test the temperature sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Once cool, test the oven temperature sensor resistance. At room temperature it should be about 1080 ohms. Replace if out of spec.

fixSteps.title: Check for stuck relay
fixSteps.instruction: With the oven off and disconnected, use a multimeter to check continuity across the bake element relay on the control board. If it shows continuity (closed) when it should be open, the relay is welded shut.

fixSteps.title: Replace the control board
fixSteps.instruction: If the relay is stuck, replace the control board. This resolves both the stuck relay and the F21 error.

whenToCallPro: F21 is a safety-critical error. If you are not confident in diagnosing the cause, call a professional immediately.
videoSearchQuery: LG oven F21 overtemperature error fix

faq.q: Is F21 dangerous?
faq.a: Yes. The oven exceeding its temperature limit can damage the appliance or surrounding cabinets. Disconnect power immediately.

faq.q: What is the maximum safe oven temperature?
faq.a: During normal operation, around 550°F. During self-clean, up to 900°F. F21 trips when the temperature exceeds the expected range for the current mode.

title: Open or Shorted Cooktop Temperature Sensor
shortDescription: A cooktop surface element temperature sensor has an open or short circuit.
fullDescription:
Error code F30 indicates a fault with a cooktop surface element temperature sensor on LG ranges that combine an oven with a cooktop. The temperature sensor under one of the burner elements has either an open circuit (broken wire) or a short circuit.

These sensors prevent the glass cooktop from overheating by monitoring surface temperature. When a sensor fails, the control board cannot regulate that burner safely and displays F30.

The affected burner may stop working or may not be limited in temperature, depending on the failure mode. This is primarily a safety issue for glass/ceramic cooktop models.

causes.cause: Loose connector at the main board

symptoms: F30 error on display
One cooktop burner does not work
Burner cycles on and off rapidly
Hot surface indicator light behaves abnormally

fixSteps.title: Power reset
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the breaker for 2 minutes and restore. If F30 returns, the sensor needs testing.

fixSteps.title: Identify the faulty burner
fixSteps.instruction: Note which burner is malfunctioning. The service manual identifies which sensor corresponds to which burner.

fixSteps.title: Test the sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power and access the underside of the cooktop. Locate the temperature sensor for the affected burner and test resistance with a multimeter.

fixSteps.title: Replace the sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Install a replacement sensor. The sensor is typically mounted with a bracket under the glass cooktop surface.

whenToCallPro: Cooktop sensor replacement requires lifting the glass top, which can be risky if not done carefully. Consider professional help.
videoSearchQuery: LG oven F30 cooktop sensor error fix

faq.q: Can I use other burners with F30?
faq.a: Yes, typically only the burner associated with the failed sensor is affected. Other burners should work normally.

faq.q: Is F30 the same as F3?
faq.a: No. F3 is the oven cavity temperature sensor. F30 is a cooktop surface element sensor.

title: Cooktop Element Shorted to Ground
shortDescription: A cooktop heating element has a short circuit to the metal chassis ground.
fullDescription:
Error code F31 indicates that a cooktop heating element has developed a short circuit to the grounded chassis of the oven. This is a potentially dangerous condition because current is flowing through an unintended path.

The most common cause is a cracked or damaged heating element where the internal resistance wire contacts the outer sheath. A damaged wire insulation touching the metal frame, or liquid seeping into the element connection, can also cause a ground fault.

This error should be taken seriously as it indicates an electrical safety issue. The ground fault may trip a GFCI outlet if one is present.

causes.cause: Cracked cooktop heating element with internal ground fault
Liquid or moisture in element connection
Control board ground fault detection circuit failure

symptoms: F31 error on display
Affected burner does not work
GFCI breaker may trip
Tingling sensation if touching the range (dangerous)

fixSteps.title: Disconnect power immediately
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the circuit breaker. A ground fault can be a shock hazard. Do not touch the range if you feel any tingling.

fixSteps.title: Inspect for visible damage
fixSteps.instruction: Lift the cooktop (if applicable) and visually inspect all heating elements for cracks, burns, or signs of damage.

fixSteps.title: Test for ground fault
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect each element and use a multimeter to test resistance between each element terminal and the chassis ground. Any reading less than infinite (OL) indicates a ground fault in that element.

fixSteps.title: Replace the faulty element
fixSteps.instruction: Replace the element showing a ground fault. Ensure new element connections are tight and insulated.

whenToCallPro: If you experience any tingling or shock from the range, do not attempt repairs yourself. Call an electrician and appliance technician.
videoSearchQuery: LG oven F31 cooktop ground fault error fix

faq.q: Is F31 a shock hazard?
faq.a: Potentially yes. A ground fault means current is flowing through the chassis. Disconnect power immediately and do not touch the range.

faq.q: Can a spill cause F31?
faq.a: A large liquid spill that reaches the element connections could cause a temporary ground fault. Allow everything to dry completely before restoring power.

title: Cooktop Element Overcurrent
shortDescription: A cooktop heating element is drawing more current than the rated maximum.
fullDescription:
Error code F32 indicates that a cooktop heating element is drawing excessive current. The control board monitors current to each element and shuts it down if the draw exceeds the rated limit. This protects the wiring, control board relays, and the element itself.

A partially shorted element that has not yet developed a full ground fault is the most common cause. The element's resistance has decreased, causing it to draw more current at the same voltage. A failing relay that supplies power to the wrong element or multiple elements simultaneously can also cause F32.

This error typically affects a specific burner and the other burners continue to function normally.
symptoms: F32 error on display
Specific burner shuts off during use
Burner may glow unevenly (bright spots)
Smell of burning from cooktop area

fixSteps.title: Power reset
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the breaker for 2 minutes. Try the affected burner at a low setting.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the element
fixSteps.instruction: Look at the affected burner for hot spots, cracks, or visible damage. A bright spot on the coil indicates a partial short.

fixSteps.title: Test element resistance
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the element and measure resistance with a multimeter. Compare to the expected value in your service manual. Lower than expected resistance confirms a partial short.

fixSteps.title: Replace the element
fixSteps.instruction: Install a replacement heating element that matches the wattage and size of the original.

whenToCallPro: If replacing the element does not resolve F32, the control board relay may be faulty and needs professional replacement.
videoSearchQuery: LG oven F32 cooktop overcurrent error fix

faq.q: Can I use other burners with F32?
faq.a: Yes, only the affected burner should be avoided. Other burners should operate normally.

faq.q: What does an uneven glow on a burner mean?
faq.a: A bright spot on a burner coil indicates a partial short circuit where resistance has decreased. The element should be replaced.

title: Oven Humidity Sensor Error
shortDescription: The oven's internal humidity sensor has malfunctioned or is reading out of range.
fullDescription:
Error code F35 indicates a fault with the oven's humidity sensor, found in LG ovens equipped with InstaView, ProBake, or EasyClean features that use humidity-based cooking. The sensor measures moisture levels inside the oven cavity to automatically adjust cooking time and temperature.

The humidity sensor can be affected by heavy grease buildup, exposure to self-clean cycle temperatures, or electrical failure. Since humidity sensing is an advanced feature, this error does not affect basic bake and broil functions.

Cleaning the sensor may resolve the issue. If the sensor element is physically damaged, replacement is required.

causes.cause: Grease or residue coating the humidity sensor
Sensor wiring damage from heat exposure

symptoms: F35 error on display
Auto-cook programs do not work correctly
Sensor-based cooking times are inaccurate
Manual bake and broil modes work fine

fixSteps.title: Power reset
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the breaker for 2 minutes and restore power.

fixSteps.title: Clean the humidity sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Locate the humidity sensor inside the oven cavity (usually on the back wall or ceiling, near the vent). Gently clean it with a damp cloth and mild detergent. Do not use abrasive cleaners.

fixSteps.title: Run a standard bake cycle
fixSteps.instruction: After cleaning, run the oven at 350°F for 20 minutes to burn off any residual moisture or cleaning agent from the sensor.

fixSteps.title: Replace the sensor if needed
fixSteps.instruction: If F35 persists after cleaning, the humidity sensor needs replacement. It is typically held by 1-2 screws inside the cavity.

whenToCallPro: If the sensor and wiring are fine but the error persists, the control board may have a faulty sensor input circuit.
videoSearchQuery: LG oven F35 humidity sensor error fix

faq.q: Can I cook normally with F35?
faq.a: Yes. Manual bake, broil, and convection modes work without the humidity sensor. Only auto-cook programs are affected.

faq.q: Does self-clean damage the humidity sensor?
faq.a: The extreme temperatures of self-clean cycles can degrade humidity sensors over time. This is one reason some manufacturers recommend limiting self-clean frequency.
